causal_chains.description: Braking too early causes low apex speed
Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner
Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application
Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 1 (54.0% – 58.1% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.535s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 1025.4 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Braking too early causes low apex speed (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: You can brake later and carry more speed into the corner — trust the car's braking capability.
    → Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: Reduce steering angle — a straighter wheel lets the car roll faster. Work on your turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback: You're carrying 21.9 km/h more speed than the expert into this segment, but unfortunately, it's not being utilized effectively. To improve, focus on braking 4.7% later than you currently are, which will allow you to maintain a better apex speed. Additionally, be mindful of your steering input, as excessive movement is scrubbing off speed through the corner. By making these adjustments, you'll be able to optimize your corner exit and ultimately gain time.

      Deviation: 948.9 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed (confidence: 99%)

    → Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner (confidence: 57%)
    💡 Tip: Reduce steering angle — a straighter wheel lets the car roll faster. Work on your turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback: You're currently running 948.9 rpm lower than the expert reconstruction, which is likely due to late throttle application. To improve, focus on getting on the throttle 5.2% earlier, which will help you maintain corner exit speed. Additionally, try to reduce your steering input by 1.5° to minimize speed scrubbing through the corner. By making these adjustments, you'll be able to carry more speed through the turn and improve your overall lap time.

zones.llm_feedback: You're carrying 16.6 km/h less speed than optimal through this segment, and the data suggests this is largely due to braking too early and scrubbing speed with excessive steering input. Additionally, focus on smoothing out your steering wheel angle by 3.6 degrees to reduce speed loss through the corner, which will allow you to get on the throttle more progressively and avoid premature throttle application.

causal_chains.description: Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed
Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex
Excess apex speed causes understeer (high lateral load)

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 5 (33.7% – 36.2% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.061s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 725.1 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed (confidence: 49%)
    💡 Tip: Get on the throttle earlier and more decisively on corner exit to maximise straight-line speed.
    → Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex (confidence: 37%)
    💡 Tip: Try initiating your braking earlier and with more initial pressure to scrub speed before the turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback: You're currently running 725.1 rpm lower than the expert reconstruction, which is likely costing you corner exit speed. To improve, focus on applying the throttle 3.4% more aggressively, aiming to get on the power sooner to take advantage of the corner exit. Additionally, consider braking 2.6% harder to reduce your excess speed at the apex, which will help mitigate the understeer you're experiencing due to high lateral load. By making these adjustments, you should be able to find more speed and stability through this segment of the track.

summary_llm: The biggest area for improvement lies in optimizing braking points and throttle application, as evidenced by the significant RPM deficits across all zones, with Zone 1 showing the largest deficit of 1025.4 rpm. To address this, focus on delaying braking by a fraction of a second in Zone 1 to increase apex speed. Despite the estimated total time loss of ~1.557s, it's encouraging to see that the issues are largely related to braking and throttle application, which are skills that can be refined with practice. By making these adjustments, you can make significant gains and improve your overall lap time.
